reviews.review_description:
The game its self has a lot of potential but it is just not very good. Everything feels really repetitive and i wish there were more spells you could unlock ad you progress in the game. The graphics aren’t all that good either and the enemy’s just all feel the same. The cards are cool but they should really add other ways to score more points in a level. Also if they added potions that could either heal you or damage your enemy that would be cool. This game has a lot of potential to bea very good game but right now it is just not that good.
￼

reviews.review_title: This is what I want from a VR game!
reviews.review_description: I love this! I'm pretty new to VR and a lot of games just feel so underdeveloped and I've avoided VR for years for that reason. This game is the kind of game I've been waiting for! It really helped me get the hang of this style of controls too. It moved along at the right pace for me to help me get good and comfortable with VR gaming
Game is good

reviews.review_title: This is how create vr wizared game
reviews.review_description: Wonderful game is very intersting to play this game espically on vr

reviews.review_title: Cool but average Dr Strange like game
reviews.review_description:
It is the kind of game we need in VR but needs more attention. The graphics and physics are quite basic with simple animations.
I'd like more spell variety and unblocking them gradually, not from the beginning. Maybe add some potions. Also, for single hands, we cannot use both hands. It would be interesting if there were some recharge time or mana bar. 
Quite repetitive and easy as we cannot change the difficulty. Consider maybe putting weaknesses or resistances to some monsters. After 5 stages, I didn't really feel like getting back. The cards concept is interesting in adding more challenges if you want to replay a level but I don't like the fact that this is the only way of getting a better score. There are collectible gems but you cannot explore freely as some doors may close behind you and you need to restart and remember not to take the wrong turn.
The game is quite short. There are 10 stages and 2 bosses. The final one doesn't feel like being one and the ending is weird.
I recommend it but it is not worth the full price.

Although graphically it is not magic (which is not bad at all anyway), everything else is. The system of use of the magic is really cool. The game is progressive and exploratory with passages to be opened and secrets to discover. Fighting (now still easy) are quite well done. I played for about an hour and liked it more and more. The price (discounted) is super honest and certainly worth more. For now 4 stars. When I take it to an end I will give a definitive judgment. Unfortunately the game is too short. 4 stars remain.
Not bad but not good
Loving this game!!!

developer_response.body:
Hi Last.Son,

Thank you for leaving a positive review! Enjoy the game! Cheers!

reviews.review_title: I just dont think it was worth it.
reviews.review_description:
Saw the game and it looked good. Was excited and played the game. I was done in about two hours. (I think) I was expecting l....oh I dont know...more. I was expectig to see myself in the past and not just a couple of levels with a whole bunch of useless powers.

Why even give us powers if the only good thing is the fireball and shield.

Now. I know game development is hard...really hard so I am sorry, but if you charge people 40 dollars for this game, you better have some more secrets than, here is a pink gem woohoo. 

Im sorry, but I am terribly dissapointed. Feel like I've been scammed on this.

developer_response.body:
Hello, thank you for sharing your thoughts!
We are sorry to hear you were disappointed with the game. We strive to offer the best experience possible, and we genuinely appreciate your input and are committed to improving. We hope you'll continue to follow our other projects and see the progress we make!

reviews.review_title: Can I please get a refund?
reviews.review_description: I accidentally bought this game a while ago well trying to find a different VR game. It looks really fun, but it’s not the game that I was looking for. Can I please get a refund?

I bought this about a year ago after reading the reviews and watching the trailer. Little did I know I was gonna have the time of my life playing this. the spells feel so good to cast. this game is a 10/10 i recommend it to anyone who likes magic and fighting cus this game takes that to a whole new level.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your amazing review! 
We're glad to hear that you had such a great time playing the game and that we could provide a fun experience. We hope you continue to have a blast with the game!

reviews.review_description: First of all this game is great. I love how the hand movements trigger different spells, which is really cool and fun to do. The plot is a bit messy, but also easy to understand. The controls are also very easy once you get used to them. A personal issue I have with this game is that when I am moving around I get a bit motion sick, but that may not apply for you.  This game is also pretty easy. I beat this game and did not die once, and I’m not that great at video games. Another bad thing about this game is the enemy’s are all pretty much the same. Besides a few bosses, the enemies are all goblins and orcs. This game was a fun experience, and this is my honest review. Thank you for your time, and I hoped I helped you make a decision to buy this game or not -WB

developer_response.body: Hey there, ApolloDragon09! First of all, we are glad you had a good time playing our game. Casting gesture-based spells in the magical world of Meliora is indeed a hell of a lot of fun! Anyway, thank you for drawing our attention to the problems you described in your review - we will surely take a closer look at them and think about implementing some improvements. Cheers!

reviews.review_title: This is the game what I want.
reviews.review_description:
It's a great game, story, puzzles, collect points to learn new magics and you can choose by your self which magic you want to learn more.
 I think you can learn all of them at the end, I didn't finish yet. Use Gestures to use magic is really cool and works well. I can do it very well during practice but during fighting I am a little bit nervous, sometimes not so good. That is exactly like I were a new wizard and being better and better. I hope in the future can PVP, even with different different roles, like warriors, monks etc. 
At the first stage I saw Dragon already,  I am motivated.  Is that the final Boss? Can't wait to meet him again.

This is the kind of game I was really looking forward to when I first got my VR. Being able to hold a fireball and shoot magic arrows is THE BEST! I'd really like to see more of an open world setting but this is an amazing start. I hope they create another game after this, there needs to be more magic in my reality!

the game is not bad but not worth 25 euros
At most 15
A blast to play! Really fun! Can get a bit confusing with the spells and stuff, and I wish they had the option to play without the controllers, and just yur hands, but overall good game!!

Just finished this game, and all I can say is wow. The game truly does make you feel like you have magical powers; my favorite power is the electricity you can shoot from your hands. I just wish that the game was longer!
Total and complete trash. Don’t waste your time or you money.
Awesome! Would have made my childhood complete if it had came out 30 years earlier:). But seriously, it's awesome

This game is great. The gestures feel just right. The locomotion feels good and I'm glad there's options for sliding and teleporting.

More about the gestures - They make it very immersive. I felt like a real wizard every time I summoned spells. The fireball started off feeling weird but soon I was chunking them like an NBA player in the 3 point contest. Walking into a new area and summoning the shield makes me feel invincible. No spoilers about the other spells, but they feel even better.

Great game so far. Definitely worth the $$.

A Great puzzle experience.  An Okay VR experience.  The levels are fun. The controls are clunky; does not follow the directions taught to us in 'First Steps'. Fails to take advantage of what the Quest can do.

Bug: completed Master Challenge to fully upgrade the Fireball spell, then died. The challenge is now incomplete, and cannot be completed. 
On its face, the game is a simple rehash of an old formula: go here, deal with this flood of enemies, move on to the next too-small room. Maybe you'll find some secret items along the way.
But the spell gestures really make it something else. You don't select your spell like it's some weapon from a menu, you have access to all six spells by their gestures. These gestures impose "cooldowns" by the amount of time they take to execute.
Where this game suffers, is with the specialist enemies. The mages make it difficult to cast anything other than Fireball, while they're bombarding you. And the Aether phantoms are total BS. Can't dodge them, and the only effective spell also prevents you from using any other magic.
This game is amazing, the controlls are really easy to learn and the graphics are amazing. I really hope for some sort of expansion because I want to see more of this game

Would say this is my worst game in my collection, regret buying this,sounded great fun but not.
The tracking seems to be off,hand disappears from time to time, teleporting not great, actually is pretty annoying to be honest.
Would like a refund to buy something else.
Graphics could have been better on giants,like a big walking blob walking towards you.
Oh well,not one for the library unfortunately in my opinion and I love this type of game but not this one.
Tracking and poor quality let this down for me.

First off, the hand gestures to create your spells is amazing. Its an awesome idea and it works very well. It really feels like you are creating a bow with magic, or a fireball, whatever else you might want. But its as if thats the onlt thing going for it. Doesnt look very good. The enemies are dumb and easy, especially since you can continue to teleport away from them, and the environment is unimaginative. Once you get over how fun it is to have the hand gestured spells, this game is dull. Definitely wish i had not spent as much money as i did on it
I didn't feel any heart in the game,

This is a great game that brings out my inner nerd, but after I finished the story, I had no want to jump back in as I do with other games. The fate cards are neat, but not something that’s going to draw me back in and only gives the  game slightly replayibility    Maybe adding in some type of arena, horde, or arcade mode might bring back in. As well as multiplayer. Bettering the graphics will also increase the appeal.

The game is great but the tracking for the gestures could use some fine-tuning. It kind of ruins the combat when you have to try to cast the same spell 10 times before it works. So far it seems to mainly be with the fireball and shield spells. The game is great other than that though.
Great mechanics

I absolutely love this game! Normally play it on Steam. Am very, very happy they have made it accessible on the Quest. :)

At the same time, they did not include Arena Mode on the Quest version, which was my favourite way to play. 

Bugs:

- When you set gaze based aim to 0, instead of it shutting off. It gets buggy sending spells in random directions/velocities.

I like the casting spell and combat system.
I completed 100% of this game in around 12hours...
Need more staff to do for sure.
I would love to see a horde coop survival mode!!!

Want to feel like Dr Strange? Here's your chance! Solid concept and well executed. Enough spell variety once they're upgraded to add slow / stun effects. Good voice acting and sound in general. Very polished.
Unique intense and hardly glitchy
The game was really cool I never imagined me shooting fire balls at goblins in cry I had no regrets at all
I love this just what ppl need if they like merlin and the like cast spells fight orca etc I say don’t think just jump right in

Yes a very good game with me putting hours into it but can U maybe add a few more speeds and better graphics cuz I feel like it's not the realistic and needs more magic stuff like potions and Mele weapons overall a good game

developer_response.body:
Hello, thank you for your feedback! 
It's great to hear your thoughts, and we’re happy you had a good time with the game overall!

reviews.review_description: As a VR newbie, this game hits the "why VR?" nail right on the head. The visuals are captivating and the gesture-based combat is excellent as you teleport about as a Sorceror taking on hordes (or a few) Gobbos. A very well put together game overall. I'd like to see more classic spells (Sleep, Magic Missile, Familiar, etc), and maybe a party mode.  Only real negative is I am still getting used to the auto aim, which seems a bit "hit and miss" at times especially if you gesture and cast too fast. However, I'd put this a joint top game alongside Space Pirate Trainer.
This game is not too hard or not too easy. I've played till my legs start hurting. I'm really happy with it!
4 sterne

Being new to the whole vr world I had found that there are a lot of games that just really don’t live up to the hype. But this game definitely has given me hope I just have to keep looking. The graphics are great and smooth enough that even when sitting to play you don’t get disoriented (unless you turn your sensitivity to max), so far the game play is really fun, the hand gesture magic use is an awesome feature that makes you wish you could really do this. Only giving 4 stars because the only issue I have is that you are scored points on each level but I have no idea how they actually score you. So if your trying to maybe beat your friends high score you have no idea what perimeters you have to do better at to score higher. 

Overall great job devs. Amazing game and I’d love to play some more of your games in future.
